environment.memory_metric: psutil peak_wset (true peak working set of the measuring subprocess, Windows). Each engine/op measurement runs in its OWN python subprocess so peaks do not contaminate each other.
environment.note: Absolute MB includes CPython + library overhead (~100-150MB floor). The portable signal is the RATIO between engines on the same machine, and the shape of the per-op table — not the absolute MB.

subject.name: Polars new streaming engine
subject.freshness_note: The streaming engine is OPT-IN, not the default, as of 1.42.1 — official docs: 'the streaming engine is not the default' / 'will become the default in time'. It has been developed over 1+ year (tracking issue pola-rs/polars#20947); do not read this run as 'just stabilized'.

dataset.note: Generated fresh by run.sh via sink_parquet (streaming write); not committed here.

determinism.note: The dataset and query checksums reproduce exactly on a pinned Polars. Peak-memory MB varies run-to-run (allocator, thread scheduling) by roughly +/-10%; the cross-engine ratios and the rows-out pattern (reducing vs row-preserving) are the durable claims. Timings are secondary and machine-dependent.

harness_provenance.note: harness/probe.py is the file executed on 2026-07-16, copied verbatim from the session workspace, with ONE edit: the module docstring's incorrect '(stabilized in 1.41.0)' claim was corrected (see docstring). The per-column float-diff breakdown in results.json (experiment B detail) was computed ad-hoc in the same session on the same outputs; probe.py's `equal` mode reproduces the headline equals=False.

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: venv + 87MB parquet deleted after the run (finite local disk)
